column1: # Values are PER ONE UNIT of the stated unit (multiply by qty). Micros are rough estimates.
# pantry: 1 = on hand. base: 1 = can START a plate. green/probiotic: functional flags. role: for recipe phrasing (base/protein/veg/green/aromatic/sour/seed/fruit/condiment/dairy).
# topper: 1 = a micro-layer item the menu generator may ADD to any slot to repair a nutrient gap (seeds/greens/sours/moringa).

column2: and only one serving per day — that is what keeps a repair edible.

column1: # shelf = days it keeps AFTER buying — decides the shopping run (<=3 fresh/daily · 4-14 weekly · >14 monthly staple).
#         it also caps how far ahead you can buy: you cannot stock a month of spinach.
# pack  = how many of `unit` come in one purchase pack. packname = what you actually ask for at the shop.
#         CAREFUL: for dals/grains `unit` is a COOKED cup but you buy a DRY packet. Convert
#         g_dry_per_cup = kcal_per_cup / (kcal_per_g_dry)  -> 1 cup cooked dal = ~35 g dry
#         Getting this wrong silently multiplies every buy (a 12-cup dal pack over-buys 2.3x).
#         all of these are estimates — tune them and re-run `python3 app.py import-csv`.
# lead = HOURS of ahead-time needed BEFORE you can cook with this (soaking
#   `effort` is cook time; `lead` is the thing effort misses. A 12-min rajma bowl is a fiction
#   if nobody soaked the rajma last night. The menu page turns this into a nightly prep queue.
# prep = what you actually do. No commas.
# makes = space-sep raw input ids this row is MADE from (soaking/sprouting/bhuna). Presence marks a
#   PREP OUTPUT — a thing you make
